author: @tiomkinnyborg2289
text: Culture, your mic sounds muffled.  How about doing a mic check and fixing it?  Things sound better and clearer when treble frequencies can get through.  To get a better sound move away from the mic and if necessary alter the volume.  You can safely use a mic from 30cms (1 foot) away.

author: @narnia1233
text:
Unfortunately I think it’s gonna take a long time before we can consistently convince the majority of young women that they aren’t living in a hand maid’s tale here in the West.  That there’s no movement for such a thing and they actually are living in a good world with people on both sides of the political isle who want them to have a great life.  

I think it’s starting to change amongst women.  I’m a woman.  And I see some change.  

But, I don’t think it’s yet become a huge shift in mindset amongst young women.  

I’m not certain how to reach the YA female demographic.  Because it’s true that I think they like female power fantasies.  Which, that’s perfectly fine because essentially it makes sense at that age.

But, in the power fantasies I think instead of pretending to be in a hand maid’s tale they should just follow a typical hero’s journey instead.  Where the female character is struggling with a personal issue—not a made up society that’s “keeping her down.”  

We used to have stories about individual struggles a female character has and they were really good.  Like issues dealing with parental divorce, issues with relationships, issues with self confidence, etc.  

We need to go back to the hero’s journey stories.  

And I think women want the hero’s journey too—they just don’t realize it.  

For example I follow some discussion boards about comics and I keep seeing women bringing up how they want stories that show a female character actually having a personal struggle.  Such as one woman recently posted that she wanted to see stories about mother characters and the struggles of raising children.  And yes, for real this was a young woman who clearly was an ultra feminist type.  

In her post she wrote how she believed it was due to misogyny and the patriarchy and etc. that we today don’t have stories of women who are mothers that struggle with parenting and showing how a character would gradually change and the family relationship repaired.  Plus she wrote how she personally didn’t want to have children but again she wanted to see a genuine mother raising kids story.  That she was tired of seeing women who didn’t have trouble being a professional, raising kids, etc. 

So, yeah, they actually do want traditional stories—they just don’t phrase it 100% accurately because they only know how to think within the bounds of the ideology they were indoctrinated on.

replies.author: @danielalpine1994
replies.text:
Which is not that different from the Pharaoh in the story of Exodus. His stubbornness and pride was the reason for his empire’s destruction. All he had to do was to free the Israelites from slavery as Moses asked which was the order of God. The Lord would sent down the Plagues but Pharaoh still wouldn’t relent. But it wasn’t until the final Plague took his son, he relented and let the slaves go. It also wouldn’t have  happened if he didn’t set out to kill every first born Hebrew child. He brought that upon himself. But even after he decided to chase after the freed slaves and kill Moses, he wouldn’t succeed after his soldiers all drowned.

All Iger and the current board had to do was cease the woke bull and to focus on entertainin people. But instead they decided to cause their own demise

author: @richardmartin8998
text: I think there is a decent chance that Ahsoka season 2, Mangold's SW movie and the Rey movie do not get made, and for 5 reasons. Both Iger and KK are in their mid 70s and are going to be moving on in the next 24 months. The Disney chairman has already rejected a number of Iger's preferred picks, and could well be running Iger's contact out to get him out. KK will be embarrassed once court hearings start in the Carano lawsuit, probably to the point where the board will fire her (she has no dirt on them remember). Second, every Star Wars project is in development hell, running over budget, under performing and being poorly received. Mando will be a failure and Filoni will be the fall guy. As a result the course correction will be easier. Three Snow White will fail badly. Coupled with Mando, Star Wars recent history, and Marvel recent history Disney will pause further development of as many projects as possible to assess viability. This already happening. Four Disney has a bucket load of debt due on purchases like Fox, Hulu as well as debt mounting across its divisions. The theme parks are a mess, Disney Plus is a black hole and their movies are loss leaders in the billions since 2019. Finally, except for California, the regulatory environment is changing for Disney, their share price is going down and their brand is tarnished. They are in decline and everyone knows it. They can't afford to appear weaker, and flop after flop, drama around KK and regulatory investigations are headaches they don't need. Added together and it seems obvious they need wins. And if it's between the board and Iger and KK, the board will back themselves. Remember folks: always back the horse called self interest.

author: @talithakoum3922
text: Frozen 1 was clearly five different rough drafts of a script slapped together at the last minute.  The only logical direction for a sequel would have been a redemption arc for Prince Hans, since he made such an unconvincing villain in the first one.  Of course, the second movie (which I never bothered to see) didn't go there, instead doubling-down on the girl boss tropes that have grown so tiresome.  It would be truly hilarious and based if, as a course correction, Disney made a redemption arc for Hans and a Hans/Elsa romance the plot of Frozen 3.  Purple-haired activist Disney adults would stream themselves crying on social media.  And the actual target audience of little girls would love it.

replies.author: @ardendragoon
replies.text: Simply. They are communists trying to destroy traditions and culture.

replies.author: @John-fk2ky
replies.text:
No offense, but if your parents taught you that way, they taught you wrong. You ALWAYS look things up when they’re the main subject being discussed. 

A very simplistic summary of Neomarxism would be that it, like Marxism, looks at the world through the dichotomy of haves and have-nots. In normal Marxism, the dividing line is between classes. It also shows a complete lack of understanding of economics and human nature, but that’s not the point here. Neomarxism, takes Marxism, flaws and all, and simply moves the dividing line from between economic classes to one between ethnicities or even between sexes. Any angle where you can divide people between oppressors and oppressed, that’s what the ideology does. Of course, in order to do this, you have to ignore a lot of inconvenient facts or else all the levels of nuance force the framework to break down.
